instructions: Assume all build and test work runs inside the Distrobox container.
Write shell and tooling instructions for the container, not the host.
Treat host-level package changes as out of scope unless explicitly required.

hard_rules: Do not implement code in the main agent.
Spawn exactly one sub-agent per step unless the step is explicitly decomposed.
Do not overlap file ownership between steps.
Do not advance until the current step's exit criteria are met.
Keep v1 limited to OpenAI-compatible chat completions, text-only, SSE streaming, and basic bearer-token auth.
orchestration_loop: Read the current step from docs/openai-phone-endpoint-plan.md.
Spawn a sub-agent for the current step.
Assign only the files and responsibilities listed for that step.
Tell the sub-agent not to revert or touch unrelated files.
Review the result against the step exit criteria.
If the step passes, continue to the next step.
If the step fails, send a precise fix request or stop if blocked.

step_order.title: Scope lock and API contract
exit_criteria: Supported OpenAI API surface is explicit.
Auth and error model are explicit.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Create or refine the v1 API contract only. Do not touch code. Freeze the supported OpenAI surface, request/response shapes, auth model, and unsupported features.

step_order.title: Add transport dependency and build plumbing
exit_criteria: Chosen HTTP server dependency is declared.
No server behavior is introduced yet.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Add the HTTP server dependency and any minimal parsing helpers needed. Do not add server behavior.

step_order.title: Extract server-facing model access
exit_criteria: A service-side caller can resolve the selected model.
A service-side caller can initialize and clean up a model.
The solution does not depend on a ViewModel.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Create a non-UI server-facing model access layer that can resolve, initialize, and clean up models without depending on a ViewModel. Keep changes minimal and isolated.

step_order.title: Add OpenAI DTOs and mapping helpers
step_order.subagent_prompt: Create OpenAI-compatible DTOs and pure mapping helpers only. No server I/O and no runtime changes.

step_order.title: Add a stateless inference gateway
exit_criteria: One request maps to one inference run.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Create the stateless inference gateway that translates one OpenAI chat request into one LiteRT-LM inference run. Serialize access and keep it separate from the UI chat session.

step_order.title: Scaffold the foreground service and lifecycle
step_order.subagent_prompt: Add the foreground service shell that owns the HTTP server and registers it in the manifest. Include lifecycle and notification scaffolding only.

step_order.title: Implement GET /v1/models
exit_criteria: PC client receives valid OpenAI-shaped models response.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Implement only the GET /v1/models route. Keep the response OpenAI-compatible and limited to servable models.

step_order.title: Implement non-streaming POST /v1/chat/completions
exit_criteria: A client can get a full completion over HTTP.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Implement only the non-streaming POST /v1/chat/completions route. Parse the request, call the inference gateway, and return the OpenAI-shaped JSON response.

step_order.title: Implement SSE streaming for POST /v1/chat/completions
allowed_changes: SSE streaming path for chat completions
step_order.subagent_prompt: Implement only the SSE streaming path for POST /v1/chat/completions. Emit OpenAI-style delta chunks and terminate with [DONE].

step_order.title: Add config, authentication, and UI control
exit_criteria: Port and bearer token are persisted.
Model selection is visible to the user.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Add server config storage and UI control for enabling, disabling, and configuring the endpoint. Keep auth simple and persistent.

step_order.title: Add tests and docs
exit_criteria: DTO mapping and request validation are covered.
step_order.subagent_prompt: Add tests and docs only. Cover DTO mapping, validation, single-flight behavior, and a short PC connection guide.
